title: Client Report - Invasive Bivalve Species in the USA
text:
Introduction




Show the code
# color pallete
bivalve_pallette <- c("5" = "#8F4444", "95" = "#060303", "92" = "#A99E26")

# plotting trend of invasive populations over the decades
ggplot(year.x.bv, aes(x=Year, y=Count, col=as.factor(Species.ID))) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = bivalve_pallette) +
  scale_y_continuous(n.breaks = 8) +
  labs(
    title = "Invasive Species Sightings Across The Years",
    y = "Detections")+
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "Null",
    axis.title.x = element_blank())+
  annotate("text", x=2003, y=300, label="Zebra Mussel", color = "#8F4444")+
  annotate("text", x=1995, y=75, label="Quagga Mussel", color = "#060303")+
  annotate("text", x=2007, y=700, label="Golden Clam", color = "#A99E26")











Show the code
# creating interactive map of invasive populations since 2020
leaflet() %>%
  addTiles(group = "OSM (default)") %>%
  addProviderTiles(providers$Esri.WorldImagery, 
                   group = "World Imagery") %>%
  setView(lng = -100, lat = 40, zoom = 3.5) %>%
  addPolygons(
    data = states,
    color = "#2E8B57", 
    weight = 3, 
    fill = FALSE,
    group = "State Borders") %>%
  addCircles(
    data = zm.map.bivalv, 
    color = "#8F4444",
    group = "Zebra Mussel",
    weight = 7) %>%
  addCircles(
    data = qm.map.bivalv, 
    color = "#060303",
    group = "Quagga Mussel",
    weight = 7) %>%
  addCircles(
    data = gc.map.bivalv, 
    color = "#A99E26",
    group = "Golden Clam",
    weight = 7) %>%
  addLayersControl(
      baseGroups = c("OSM (default)", "World Imagery"),
      overlayGroups = c(
      "State Borders",
      "Zebra Mussel",
      "Quagga Mussel",
      "Golden Clam"),
      options = layersControlOptions(collapsed = FALSE)
    )








Show the code
# pivot count dataset for presenting
pivot.bivalv <- count.bivalv %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Common.Name, values_from = Count)

# create table of counts
pander(pivot.bivalv)

title: Calvin Borrowman’s CV
text:
Former Field Inspector and Student of Bio-Informatics.





Standing on the shoulders of giants


Genetics, Bio-Informatics, and Coding.



Invasive species populations, genetics, and identifying diseases.




2021 - now Brigham Young University of Idaho, Rexburg.
















2025 Idaho State Department of Agriculture, Boise, ID

Field Inspector
Inspected for diseased plants in seed fields

2021 Milk Logic, Nampa, ID

Lab Tech
Plated milk samples
Picked up supplies and samples

title: about me
text:
My name is Calvin Park Borrowman and I’m a student of BYU-Idaho. I’m currently studying to get a bachelors degree in bio-informatics. What I strive to do with my degree is to pursue a career in biomedical research.
Furthermore, this portfolio serves as is to demonstrate my ability as a data scientist in the field of bio-informatics. Throughout this website is a collage of my data wrangling and computational skills, which show personal examples of my qualifications in data science.
